answer.en: A JSON Formatter (also called a JSON beautifier or pretty printer) takes compact or minified JSON and adds whitespace and indentation to make it human-readable.

question.en: What is the difference between formatting and beautifying JSON?

answer.en: They are the same thing. Both terms describe adding indentation and newlines to JSON to improve readability.
No. JSON must be syntactically valid before it can be formatted. Use the JSON Validator to find and fix any errors first.
You can choose 2 spaces (most common), 4 spaces, or a tab character for indentation.
Yes. All formatting happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your JSON is never sent to any server.

description.en: All processing runs locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to any server.
Get results immediately — no waiting for server processing.

No subscription, no limits, no hidden costs. Free forever.
